title: signal-brief-composer schemas
description: Input `signal` objects come from the analysis layer; the skill emits a `brief`. Numbers in the brief must trace to fields here — the skill phrases, it never computes.

type.description: Closed taxonomy. Drives the accent color and the category tag.

pain.description: The problem stat that opens the title.

value.description: The upside of acting — becomes the second half of the title. amount may be null; if so the title uses a qualitative value and NO number is invented.

range.description: Preferred over false precision for projections, e.g. '~$1.6M' or '+12–18 pts'.

basis.description: How the figure was derived. Required so the projection is auditable.

story.description: One- to two-sentence cause from the analysis. Must name its own subject (used to keep the body self-contained).

recommended_action.description: What the PRD does. The skill never invents scope beyond this.

prd_ref.description: If null, the CTA becomes 'Draft PRD' instead of 'View PRD'.

prototype_ref.description: If null, the CTA becomes 'Generate prototype' instead of 'View prototype'.

sources.description: Tools/signals that fed this. Render honestly — one source means one chip.

evidence.description: Concrete datapoints. At least one required to clear the surfacing gate.

confidence.description: Below the floor (default 0.6) the signal is suppressed.

greeting.description: Max 3 lines. Offensive framing. Totals must equal the sum of figures in `cards`.

accent.description: Hex from the taxonomy table; must match the type and the valence.

body.description: Self-contained, max 4 lines, arc = why → worth → review-and-approve.

signal_id.description: Backlink to the source signal — supports the audit trail.

suppressed.description: Signals deliberately not shown, with reason. Supports dismissal memory and the audit trail.
